name: EU Horizon Europe Research and Innovation Action (RIA)
funding_body: European Commission — Horizon Europe Framework Programme
emphasis: Three pillars evaluated with equal weight: (1) Excellence — scientific quality, methodology, innovation beyond state of the art; (2) Impact — measurable EU-level outcomes, dissemination, exploitation, KPIs; (3) Implementation — consortium quality, work packages, risk management, budget justification. European Added Value is a cross-cutting criterion — the proposal must explain why this requires EU-level funding rather than national funding. Tie explicitly to at least one Horizon Europe Mission or cluster priority.
avoid: Single-institution proposals — consortium of at least 3 partners from different EU member states or associated countries is standard. Purely basic research without societal impact pathway. Vague KPIs. Budget without detailed justification per work package.
rhetorical_tone: Policy-aware, impact-forward, collaborative. Use work package (WP) language. Numbered deliverables and milestones are expected.

sections.name: Excellence
sections.notes: Cover: objectives and ambition, methodology and approach, level of innovation, open science practices. Must reference and position against state of the art explicitly.

sections.name: Impact
sections.notes: Cover: expected outcomes and impacts (quantified KPIs where possible), dissemination and communication plan, exploitation strategy, European Added Value statement.

sections.name: Implementation
sections.notes: Cover: work plan with numbered WPs and tasks, Gantt chart, consortium partner descriptions and roles, budget breakdown per partner and WP, risk register with mitigation strategies.

sections.name: Ethics
sections.notes: Complete the ethics self-assessment table even for low-risk proposals. Omitting this section causes administrative rejection. If human data, AI, or dual-use technology is involved, provide detailed justification.

notes: Horizon Europe uses a two-stage evaluation: remote expert review followed by consensus panel. The Impact section is frequently underwritten by applicants from pure-science backgrounds — invest proportionate effort in it.
